author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

The renderer at `crates/jjf/src/main.rs:2410` was emitting the
wire spelling of `DepKind::as_str` (`blocks`, `parent-child`,
...) verbatim as the label under `dependencies:`. Wire spelling
describes the edge in isolation, but the displayed issue IS
the owner — so the label has to read from the owner's
perspective. Fix shipped in `aa121ccb`.

Added `DepKind::as_show_label` on `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`
returning `"blocked by"` / `"parent"` / `"related"` /
`"discovered from"`. Mapping choices: `blocked by` is the
load-bearing fix from the ticket. For `parent-child` I picked
plain `parent:` over `parent of:` — the issue's own perspective
on the edge is "my parent is X", and `parent: <id>` reads as
"my parent is `<id>`" without the awkward extra preposition.
`related:` and `discovered from:` keep close to the wire form.

Wire surface unchanged everywhere it actually carries semantics:
JSON output, `Jjf-Dep-Kind:` trailers, `--kind` flag values,
`dep tree`, and the `dep add`/`dep rm` confirmation lines all
still use `as_str`.

Smoke-verified against the planner: `./bin/jjf show db53a3e`
now prints `blocked by: 52ee82d` under `dependencies:`, and
`./bin/jjf show db53a3e --json | jq '.dependencies'` still
emits `[{"target":"52ee82d","kind":"blocks"}]`.

# Recommendation

Land as-is and close. Doc updated in `docs/cli-json.md` §`jjf
show plain-text dependency section`; spec/storage shapes
untouched.

# Confidence

High. Workspace tests green (587 pass, was 583 — +4 from this
ticket: 2 unit on `DepKind`, 2 integration on show text/JSON).
Existing `dep_add_default_kind_blocks_show_reports_edge`,
`dep_add_all_four_kinds_round_trip`, `dep_rm_removes_only_named_kind`,
`new_with_inline_dep_kind_syntax`, and the three `!contains(...)`
negative-assertion sites in the validation tests were updated
to expect (or assert absence of) the new labels.

# Open follow-ups

None for this bug. If a future ticket adds new dep kinds, both
`as_str` and `as_show_label` need to be extended — they're
intentionally two separate methods so the wire form can't drift.
